text_0011: This outpost was built decades ago to seal the cursed cave. Rusty weapons and scattered military journals lie everywhere.

text_0014: You leave the military fort and return to the northern road area.

text_0020: You carry no food to offer.

text_0025: Puzzle: The three seal locations are forest graveyard altar. Enter the first letter of each rune in uppercase order.

text_0028: Wrong code. Poison gas leaks and injures you.
text_0029: Deliver all three garrison journals to the colonel at the top headquarters to uncover hidden historical truth.
text_0031: Return to me once you have collected all three military garrison journals. I will reveal the official record of the cave seal.
text_0032: You submit all journals. The colonel narrates the sealed history of your family's eternal guardianship.
text_0033: You obtain classified archive files which unlock a hidden military ending route.

text_0040: The tribal totem has already been retrieved.

text_0042: The tribe refuses to communicate until their stolen totem is returned.
text_0043: You attack the tribe and seize gold treasures, but gain heavy evil karma. All orcs will stay hostile in subsequent playthroughs.
text_0046: A primordial titan sleeps deep inside the illusion tunnel, witnessing the birth of the ancient sealing curse.

text_0049: All your accumulated evil karma is completely cleansed by ancient holy light.

text_0054: The wizard built this place to seal his most dangerous experiments.

text_0064: 'First the one that wakes the field,

text_0077: He freezes when he hears you, then slowly turns around.
text_0078: Type talk to talk, you should talk for more times.
text_0079: Bryn: Back off! This place belongs to the wizard. No trespassers allowed.
text_0080: He picks up a small wrench, looking both nervous and fierce.
text_0082: What a joke! Kill by a tiny stone!
text_0083: You tell him you are the wizard's descendant, here to end the curse.
text_0084: Bryn stares at you for a long time. His hands start to shake.
text_0085: Bryn: ...You have his eyes. Really his blood.
text_0086: Bryn: Two hundred years. I waited two hundred years for someone to come.
text_0087: Bryn: Sit. I'll tell you everything he never wrote in the diaries.
text_0088: Bryn looks up from his work. What do you want to know?
text_0089: You can ask: wizard / curse / himself / outside / dwarf / hints / upgrade / help him leave / steal from him
text_0090: Bryn: He was never a hero. He was just a man scared of losing his wife.
text_0091: Bryn: Everyone said he sealed her to save the world. Lies.
text_0092: Bryn: He sealed her because he couldn't bear to let her die, even as a monster.
text_0093: Bryn: Foolish man. Spent his whole life building a cage for the person he loved.
text_0094: Bryn: The curse didn't start with her. It started with the dwarven relic he stole.
text_0095: Bryn: Gimble thought he could use it to cure her. It only made things worse.
text_0096: Bryn: That's why the dwarf is down there, guarding the tomb. He blames us all.
text_0097: Bryn: ...To be honest, he's not entirely wrong.
text_0098: Bryn: I? I was his chief machinist. Built all the traps, all the seals, all the puzzles.
text_0099: Bryn: He told me to guard the archive until the heir arrived. So I stayed.
text_0100: Bryn: Didn't even need magic. Gnome lives are long enough to wait out a human curse.
text_0101: He laughs bitterly, tapping his cane on the stone floor.
text_0102: Bryn: Gimble. Used to be my best friend. Best blacksmith under the mountain.
text_0103: Bryn: He came here to help us. Then the relic corrupted him.
text_0104: Bryn: Now he's just an angry thing guarding the tomb. Doesn't remember anyone.
text_0105: Bryn: If you can ... don't kill him. He's suffered enough.
text_0107: The gnome seems to be quiet, do not answer you, just looking at you.
text_0108: Bryn: Hints cost faith energy. Which puzzle are you stuck on?
text_0110: Bryn: Think about the sky. What comes first, what comes after?
text_0111: Bryn: Sun wakes the world, moon guards the night, star leads the lost, void ends all.
text_0112: Bryn: Not enough faith. Go pray at the altar first.
text_0113: Bryn takes your lamp, scribes tiny runes on the glass.
text_0114: Bryn: There. Won't run out of oil anymore. Don't break it.
text_0115: Bryn: And ... be careful down there. Darkness eats people like you.
text_0116: Bryn: You need a lamp and a steady heart. You're not ready yet.
text_0117: You break the sealing rune on the workshop door.
text_0118: Bryn stares at the open passage for a long time. He doesn't move at first.
text_0119: Bryn: I ... I thought I'd die down here.
text_0120: He takes a small toolkit from his belt and hands it to you.
text_0121: Bryn: Take this. All my best work. It'll protect you from traps.
text_0122: Bryn: I'm going up. To see the sun again.
text_0123: He then go into a small tunnal.
text_0124: Bryn: The seal only responds to a pure heart. You still carry too much darkness.
text_0125: You sneak toward his toolbox while he's not looking.
text_0126: He slams his hammer down right beside your hand.
text_0127: Bryn: I knew it. Just like him. Greedy. Always greedy.
text_0128: The wall behind you slides shut. He vanishes into a side tunnel.
text_0129: You hear his voice from the dark: 'Find your own way out.'
text_0130: You don't see any tools to steal yet.
text_0131: Bryn: Really? Attack a poor old gnome? You are so evil!
text_0132: You tell him a silly joke about ghosts.
text_0133: He snorts, then chuckles, then laughs out loud — a rough, rusty sound.
text_0134: Bryn: Hundreds of years. First joke I've heard.
text_0135: He tosses you a small healing crystal.
text_0136: You hand him your food. He stares at it like it's a treasure.
text_0137: Bryn: Fresh bread. I almost forgot what it tastes like.
text_0138: He eats slowly, very slowly. His eyes soften.
text_0139: Bryn: Alright. Ask me anything. I'll give you one free hint.
text_0140: You have no food to give him.
